title: Listening - Part 3: Watching Online Videos
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about online videos they watch regularly. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for watching them. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about online videos they watch regularly. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for watching them. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about online videos they watch regularly.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: My headphones stopped working properly just after the guarantee ended. I nearly threw them away, because I assumed repairing electronic items required specialist knowledge. Then I found a video showing the same model. [19]The presenter explained exactly how to replace a loose cable, and following his instructions meant I fixed mine instead of buying another pair.[/19] Since then I've watched similar videos for a broken lamp and a bicycle light. I still avoid dangerous jobs, but many small repairs are easier than I imagined.||Speaker 2 Man: Whenever my friends and I plan a weekend away, we find dozens of websites listing the same famous attractions. Those lists don't really show whether a place would suit us. [20]I prefer videos made by travellers who walk through towns and show markets, beaches and quiet areas, because they help me decide where I'd genuinely like to spend time.[/20] Last month we chose a seaside village after seeing one online, and it was every bit as peaceful as the video suggested.||Speaker 3 Woman: I’m studying chemistry, and some parts of the course seemed impossible when I looked only at my notes. I attended lessons, but by revision time I'd forgotten why some formulas worked. [21]Before tests, I watch short videos that go through exam-style questions step by step, allowing me to pause and repeat explanations until I understand the method.[/21] They don't save me from practising, but they make revision far less frustrating. My last test result was much better than my earlier ones.||Speaker 4 Man: There is a woman who uploads weekly videos about restoring an old house. I have no intention of doing building work myself, and I'm not particularly interested in choosing paint colours. [22]I continue watching because she has such a cheerful, honest way of talking; even when something goes wrong, her reactions make me laugh and I want to know what happens next.[/22] My brother finds this strange, but for me it is like following a friendly television character rather than learning about houses.||Speaker 5 Woman: I started going to the gym recently, but some of the equipment confused me, and I worried that copying other people might result in an injury. Paying for a personal trainer every week wasn't possible. [23]I found videos by a qualified fitness instructor who demonstrates each movement slowly and explains how to avoid putting pressure on the wrong part of the body.[/23] I now plan my gym session before I go, and I feel much more confident that I'm training sensibly.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: My headphones stopped working properly just after the guarantee ended. I nearly threw them away, because I assumed repairing electronic items required specialist knowledge. Then I found a video showing the same model. The presenter explained exactly how to replace a loose cable, and following his instructions meant I fixed mine instead of buying another pair. Since then I've watched similar videos for a broken lamp and a bicycle light. I still avoid dangerous jobs, but many small repairs are easier than I imagined.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: Whenever my friends and I plan a weekend away, we find dozens of websites listing the same famous attractions. Those lists don't really show whether a place would suit us. I prefer videos made by travellers who walk through towns and show markets, beaches and quiet areas, because they help me decide where I'd genuinely like to spend time. Last month we chose a seaside village after seeing one online, and it was every bit as peaceful as the video suggested.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: I’m studying chemistry, and some parts of the course seemed impossible when I looked only at my notes. I attended lessons, but by revision time I'd forgotten why some formulas worked. Before tests, I watch short videos that go through exam-style questions step by step, allowing me to pause and repeat explanations until I understand the method. They don't save me from practising, but they make revision far less frustrating. My last test result was much better than my earlier ones.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: There is a woman who uploads weekly videos about restoring an old house. I have no intention of doing building work myself, and I'm not particularly interested in choosing paint colours. I continue watching because she has such a cheerful, honest way of talking; even when something goes wrong, her reactions make me laugh and I want to know what happens next. My brother finds this strange, but for me it is like following a friendly television character rather than learning about houses.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: I started going to the gym recently, but some of the equipment confused me, and I worried that copying other people might result in an injury. Paying for a personal trainer every week wasn't possible. I found videos by a qualified fitness instructor who demonstrates each movement slowly and explains how to avoid putting pressure on the wrong part of the body. I now plan my gym session before I go, and I feel much more confident that I'm training sensibly.

options.text: to learn how to exercise safely
to find ideas for places to visit
to follow someone whose personality they like
to learn how to prepare food
to keep up with a favourite sport

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker uses instructional videos to mend items rather than replace them (evidence [19]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Travel videos help the speaker select destinations by showing what places are really like (evidence [20]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The speaker uses videos to revise chemical methods and practise for tests (evidence [21]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The speaker chiefly enjoys the video-maker's entertaining and likeable character (evidence [22]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The speaker uses videos from a qualified instructor to perform gym exercises correctly and safely (evidence [23]).
